title: oxalis gyrorhiza
intro: Oxalis gyrorhiza, also known as the golden oxalis or wheel-flower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the wood sorrel family. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. Oxalis gyrorhiza has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and is also believed to have diuretic and expectorant properties. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture and can also be used in topical applications, making it a popular choice in herbal medicine.
